data.description:
## Context

The REVIEW-DEFENSE protocol (`reviewer-base.md:209-220`) authorizes reviewers to "lower severity or remove finding" when they "agree" with an in-code defense. The acceptance criterion ("verifiable artifacts") is subjective; a single LLM reviewer can self-override without external attestation. Inline `# isolation-ok:` comments (`reviewer-delta-standard.md:35`) operate identically — the reviewer can only check the comment exists, not whether the rationale is valid.

This is the same failure-mode class as Pattern 5 (LLM-behavioral self-attestation) but at the code-review layer instead of the orchestrator layer. The empirical proof is ticket `3dae-af1a` (open, CLI_user-tagged) where an agent satisfied a "logged rationale" requirement by self-narration. The same risk exists every time a reviewer accepts a `# REVIEW-DEFENSE:` comment with no external citation.

See `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md` Pattern 5 and `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md` G5.

## Cited evidence

**Verbatim quotes confirmed (G5):**
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-base.md:209-220`:
    > "When you encounter a `# REVIEW-DEFENSE:` comment in the code:
    >  1. Read the defense. Does it reference verifiable artifacts (code, tests, ADRs, documented patterns)?
    >  2. If you agree: lower severity or remove finding; note acceptance in description.
    >  3. If you disagree: maintain severity; explain why the defense is insufficient."
- `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-delta-standard.md:35` — `# isolation-ok:` annotation: reviewer can only check the comment exists; criteria for valid rationale unspecified

**Empirical failure mode:**
- `3dae-af1a` (open, CLI_user-tagged): brainstorm agent satisfied "logged rationale" requirement by self-narration, skipped scrutiny pipeline, applied `brainstorm:complete` tag. Same self-attestation mechanism applies to reviewer downgrades.
- Memory entry `feedback_review_gate_discipline.md`: documents prior incident where review-gate diff hash was rationalized around; same risk class

**Audit findings from skill-prose review:**
- Of 8 originally cited "permissive" skill clauses, 6 were controlled fallbacks with logging requirements. The 2 that survived adversarial scrutiny were both at the reviewer layer: REVIEW-DEFENSE protocol and `# isolation-ok:` annotation. See `docs/pattern-gap-review-4-26.md` Part 3 for details.
- This epic targets specifically the 2 surviving exploitable-permissiveness instances at the review layer.

## Mitigations included

- **G5-M1** — `# REVIEW-DEFENSE:` comments must cite a verifiable artifact (path, URL, ticket ID); pre-commit hook `check-review-defense-citations.sh` scans for the comment pattern and rejects entries lacking a citation token. Reviewers are instructed to NOT downgrade a finding when defense cites no artifact, regardless of how persuasive the prose.
- **G5-M2** — Two-reviewer rule for downgrades: when a deep-tier review downgrades a finding via REVIEW-DEFENSE, dispatch a second reviewer (peer specialist or arch agent) to independently evaluate the same defense without seeing the first reviewer's verdict. If second reviewer disagrees, higher severity wins. Implemented as a step inside `REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md` triggered by `severity_lowered: true` in `reviewer-findings.json`.
- **G5-M3** — Replace inline `# isolation-ok:` comments with entries in a tracked registry `plugins/dso/config/isolation-exceptions.yaml` requiring `{file, reason, approved_by, ticket_id, expires_on}` fields. Comments in code reduce to `# isolation-ok: see registry`. Pre-commit hook reads registry and refuses commits whose isolation-ok references have expired or lack approver attestation.

## Proposed success criteria

1. `# REVIEW-DEFENSE:` comments lacking a citation token are rejected at pre-commit; comments with citations are unblocked
2. Reviewer JSON output adds `severity_lowered: bool` and `defense_cited_artifact: string`; when `severity_lowered` is true, `REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md` dispatches a second reviewer
3. Inline `# isolation-ok:` annotations are deprecated; one-time migration converts existing instances to registry entries (one ticket per migrated entry to assign approver attestation retroactively)
4. Registry entries with `expires_on` in the past block the relevant commit until renewed
5. `review-stats.sh` reports downgrade rate and defense-citation rate; baseline measured pre-rollout
6. Test coverage: fixture cases verifying (a) uncited defense rejected, (b) cited defense accepted, (c) second reviewer dispatched on downgrade, (d) higher severity wins on disagreement, (e) expired isolation-ok blocks commit

## Coordination notes

- Companion to **New Epic B**: B *adds* rubric items so findings are surfaced; C *constrains* how findings are dismissed
- Interacts with `c183-ed2a` (adversarial review of inference decisions): the second-reviewer pattern in G5-M2 mirrors c183-ed2a's red-team challenge mode but applies to reviewer downgrades rather than orchestrator inferences. Implementation can reuse the dispatch infrastructure.
- Migration in SC3 will surface a one-time bulk of remediation tickets; scope-bound by current `# isolation-ok:` count (small, audit at planning time)

## References

- `docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md` Pattern 5
- `docs/process-gaps-4-26.md` G5 (verified verbatim)
- `docs/pattern-gap-review-4-26.md` Part 3 (the 2 surviving exploitable instances after adversarial scrutiny)
- `docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md` G5-M1, G5-M2, G5-M3
- `docs/new-epics-4-26.md` § New Epic C
data.title: Reviewer override discipline: defense citations, downgrade quorum, isolation-ok registry
